_note: Real numbers from results/exp2_results.json (mechanistic recall), exp3_results.json (any-backbone), probe_results.json (forget-probe). Reviewed by: python -m paperforge review --repo ../hola
title: HOLA — exact KV-cache hippocampus on a compressive recurrent state
hypothesis: The recall lift is shown on a synthetic, untrained MQAR task; the cache is perfect only until facts exceed its capacity w, and the benefit shrinks when the backbone already recalls well. Whether the lift transfers to a trained LM at larger scale is untested.

claims.claim: An exact importance KV-cache lifts associative recall far above the bare compressive recurrent state.
claims.metric: MQAR exact recall (importance cache vs state-only)
claims.regime: synthetic MQAR, 8 facts within cache capacity w=16, no training, 200 trials
claims.notes: recall is perfect within capacity; at 24/32/48 facts (beyond w=16) it degrades to 0.669/0.506/0.341 — the cache helps most exactly when facts approach/exceed capacity.

claims.claim: The importance cache generalizes past Gated DeltaNet to other linear-attention backbones.
claims.metric: MQAR recall with cache vs state-only on the Gated DeltaNet backbone (32 facts)
claims.regime: synthetic MQAR, 32 facts, no training, backbones {gdn, deltanet, gla}
claims.notes: lift is large on gdn (+0.90) and deltanet (+0.78) but only +0.12 on gla, which already recalls 0.866 state-only — the cache helps least when the state is already good.

claims.claim: Surprise (the paper's importance signal beta*||e||) predicts which facts the recurrent state forgets.
claims.metric: AUC of surprise predicting forgetting (>0.5 = predictive; the paper only asserts this)
claims.regime: forget-probe on 6144 facts, Gated DeltaNet layer 0
claims.notes: AUC is statistically indistinguishable from chance, so the probe does not support the paper's assertion. (The importance cache does cover 0.233 of forgetting failures vs recency's 0.065 — a separate, weaker claim that does hold.)
